item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: By teleporting, it 'opens a door' to somewhere else; since 1455 itself is a door, it is reluctant to use another door, creating a "door-ception" unless necessary

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shitty articles get deleted and their numbers get re-used. I don't particularly remember what "SCP-1022" was in December 2011, but I assume it had something to do with doors that opened into the wrong places. Maybe it's in an old Deletion thread, but those weren't always as detailed as they are now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How does a lab coat that lets you see microbes relate to a hopping reality warping door?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the hopping door, and the description in the comments is really good. However, other than that, the best parts are what's not in the article: it's a bland, generic, anomaly if you ignore the comments, and the expungement is terrible.
Janus is a good door, but that's not in the article; sorry but -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm glad the speculation was expunged, but frankly you just drop it entirely. Descriptions aren't for speculation.

That said, I find myself wondering what constitutes a suitable door for the second phase. Are there any patterns in its behavior?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Down for bad expungement

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necromancer here, How exactly is this like 249? The only thing they have consistently in common is that they're both doors.

But Janus is the best door.
Because Janus is a good door.

Good door.

Good. Door.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's just too little revealed in the actual article; I love the explanation given in the comments but you shouldn't have to read the comments to understand the article. As is, it reads too much like SCP-249.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gnargh.

On the one hand, the hopping door image is hilarious, the containment procedures interesting, and the explanation given in the comments is compelling.

On the other, there's some atrocious expungement, the "teleports away from those who interfere" seems arbitrary, and the explanation is in the comments, with no way to reach it from the text of the article itself.

I want to both upvote it and downvote it simultaneously, so neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get the theory that explains the expungements and the nature of the door in the comments and know where you have been heading. But SCPs that need to have the comments read for people to get it aren't really my cup of tea. Without reading the comments, the only thing I remembered was a cute hopping door, interesting containment procedures and terrible expungements. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came, I read, I upvoted.

Also, I'm waiting for a tale to come out where someone learns of it's true nature and a cult worshipping "Janus the Door" crops up.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Door god, that's a bad joke.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In this particular case, the [DATA EXPUNGED] has already been explained.

The first [DATA EXPUNGED] = "something telepathic which the Foundation doesn't want to admit to having".

The second [DATA EXPUNGED] = "it's probably one of those lobotomized gods we heard about".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A shade [data expunged] but I still like Janus the door. +1

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Mild necro, but thirded

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds me of the doors aboard the Heart of Gold in HHGTTG.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's bad enough you people have made me feel sympathy for spiders, but making me sympathize with a freaking DOOR is a new low. I hate you all.*

*not really

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just have to say, I liked the SCP okay, but was staying neutral. And then I read the discussion, and I am upvoting. Because Janus is a good door.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded:
I really wish this came through the article better, because it's amazing. But as it stands, it's just too much expungement and JanusWeena Mercator as the Hopping Door.

Sit, Janus, sit. Good door.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded:
God dammit.
I just saw this. It is awesome.
GOD DAMMIT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: raises hand.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because Janus is a good door.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If Janus wasn't so completely brainwashed, your situation might happen. But DOORS BELONG IN DOORFRAMES, JANUS IS A DOOR, JANUS BELONGS IN PRE-EXISTING DOORFRAMES, JANUS BELONGS IN PRE-EXISTING DOORFRAMES, JANUS BELONGS IN PRE-EXISTING DOORFRAMES!!!!!!

Janus doesn't dare install itself anywhere that didn't already have a door.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For some reason this entry reminds me of The Luggage from Terry Pratchett's Discworld, and that makes me want to see this instead: instead of hopping around, the door falls out of its door frame onto its "face," then hundreds of tiny legs emerge from beneath & send the door scurrying like a giant door-shaped millipede towards some new location. Instead of just teleporting (I hate hate hate when SCPs can "just teleport" for some reason) it's capable of installing itself into any location temporarily & then opening itself, creating a new doorway through which the door can then pass once it's unhooked itself again. The millipede legs allow the door to scale vertical surfaces (and indeed, even install itself into the ceiling). This creates a containment nightmare because if the door gets too bored and installs itself on the wrong wall to escape, it could open itself into some other SCP's containment facility, allowing the SCP inside to escape through the doorway that "Janus" leaves behind.

Granted, this might kinda screw with the whole "linking un-linked locations" concept, but maybe the doorways that Janus leaves behind start to experience quantum decay (I dunno, it sounds good) and become more and more unstable as time goes by. Passing through any doorway once it's begun to decay may send you to the correct location, or it may send you to any previously created doorway that has not experienced full quantum collapse yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's an SCP of scooby-doo doors and cooridors.

Upvoted because this amuses me greatly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this! Main article? Hilarious! Story in comments? Hilariously AWESOME! Seriously, this is a beautiful piece of work. I would marry it, if marrying written words was legal in America. Maybe someday, when we are wiser…

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded:
And because Janus doesn't want the other door to be punished for vacating its doorframe

You have managed to make a cute door.

My reaction to this: squeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeeee!!!

+1'd.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: You know, we have -several- SCPs that could be explained in the same way. Divinities or demons cast into the roles of objects, which they perform just slightly out of tune with what should be correct.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Seconded.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Door doesn't even look like a word to me anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Same here. The general concept holds merit, but the hopping around just smacks of Sorcerer's Apprentice silliness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Raises hand

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded:
…It took me about 4 reads to get this comment.

You are an awful, awful person.

(poor door :( )

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I picture it making the noises from "QBert" … (raise your hand if you are old enough to have ever seen a QBert machine, heh.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally, I imagine it making a "jumping-sound" video game sounds every time it hops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For some reason I can't stop picturing that scene with some sort of "Wheeeee" sound over it.

But alas, doors do not "Wheeee"…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon transition to its ambulatory phase, SCP-1455 will animate, unhook itself from its hinges, and begin to hop around its environment.

This is what makes the article for me. +1

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: So when one works for the Foundation, one does just walk into poor door?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is so much better than I would have expected.

Also, a little note on technique here that I want to call out:

This implies [DATA EXPUNGED], which is considered probable, but for obvious reasons cannot be verified.

Normally I'd be grumbling about abuse of data expungement, but the way you phrase the last part of the sentence saves it. Sure, we still don't know what the fuck is going on (unless we read the comments page of course), but it sounds interesting.

Also also:

SCP-1455 will transition to its ambulatory phase

this phrase makes me so happy you do not even know

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded:
I — buh — awesome

SECONDING "awesome"

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Do it.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Well done.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: WRITE A TALE. RIGHT THIS INSTANT.

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: So, with extensive door therapy, could Janus regain his understanding, or perhaps become a nice frying pan instead? Upvoted for interesting idea. (I personally like the idea that the Foundation has lots of things that are not trying to kill everybody, but are instead just weird and anomalous enough that normal humanity would have problems as a result of knowing they exist.)

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: I — buh — awesome

item.title: Re: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded: Nice.

item.title: EXPLANATION
item.content:encoded:
Here's what's actually happening - and although the Foundation has some clues about what's happening (that's what's behind the EXPUNGED), I can't think of a way for them to have figured this out in total.

This is not a door. It only thinks it's a door.

This is a tremendously powerful creature (let's call it "Janus" for the sake of this talk page) with innate space-bending abilities… and some entity that was even more powerful came along and fucked with its head. Lobotomized it, and brainwashed what was left real good. Made it think it's a door. (Is this revenge? Petty malice? An experiment? A game? Something we can't begin to understand?)

So, Janus is squatting there, polymorphed into a door, and thinking the only thought it's allowed to think now, which is "I'M A DOOR"

over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over and over

"I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR"

for all eternity.

But Janus still has enough personality to get bored and lonely.

Janus gets lonely when it's not used often enough, and even when it is used enough, it still gets bored. So when it's bored enough, it animates — no one ever said doors can't move, just that they don't move, so that must mean it's okay for it to move sometimes! It's not a conscious justification, but that's essentially the logic — and unhooks itself from its hinges, and hops off to stretch its metaphorical legs

…. and that's the point at which the negative-reinforcement brainpatterns kick in.

you are a door, you are a DOOR, YOU ARE A DOOR, DOORS BELONG IN DOORFRAMES, DOORS CONNECT TWO SPACES TO EACH OTHER, ANYTHING THAT CONNECTS TWO SPACES TO EACH OTHER IS A DOOR, YOU CONNECT TWO SPACES, YOU ARE A DOOR YOU ARE A DOOR YOU ARE A DOOR

…Janus cannot weep with guilt and terror, because in this form it has neither eyes nor tear ducts, and it doesn't have enough musculature to tremble like a dog waiting to be kicked, but it knows that it's being bad, and it has to get itself into a new doorframe very quickly because DOORS BELONG IN DOORFRAMES.

So Janus hops around for as long as it dares, and finds a doorframe that "looks nice" in terms of the spaces it separates from each other (we could never understand the criteria it uses; they seem to vary from month to month), and then installs itself in the doorframe. And because Janus doesn't want the other door to be punished for vacating its doorframe, it teleports the other door into the newly-vacated doorframe.

The "lag" effect is the result of Janus being very, very confused (because of the lobotomy and brainwashing). Even during its 'standard' phase, Janus teleports everyone who uses it; it's just that they're null teleports. If a gate teleports you to exactly where you were going to be anyway, you don't notice. And even when Janus is in a new doorframe, it still keeps teleporting people to the other side of the old doorframe.

But as the days go by, and the only thing Janus is allowed to think is "I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR I'M A DOOR", the memories of the spaces attached to the old doorframe become harder to keep hold of, until they eventually slip out of its grasp and it's just easier to use the spaces attached to the new doorframe.

Good door. Good door. Good door. Good door. Good door. Good door. Good door. Good door.

Good door.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed with the agreement. Somehow the actual physical door detaching feels less realistic to me than it merging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A tad heavy on vague expunging near the end, but generally a good page. I agree with posts above suggesting it should merge with doors instead of replacing them, but it's already good enough for a slightly hesitant upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The last paragraph is kinda needless, but overall I like cute hopping door. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, that's a good point. Okay. It moves at a normal speed, and if you try to interfere with it, it just teleports away towards an area with more accessible doorframes.

Why it bothers to hop if it can teleport is open for speculation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't much care for how fast it moves and how well it hides its movements. Just have it move at a normal pace, not uber-fast and uber-perceptively.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love how this one takes two overdone SCP object tropes (indestructibility, randomly moving on its own), and finds a way to make both of them new and interesting. That alone makes this worth the upvote. The only problem is that's the only thing this one really has to offer for me, otherwise it's just a slightly less interesting 249. Some sort of additional twist or consequence of its abilities would make this go from decent to extremely good, in my book.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with that. Then it makes sense if the sample retrieved is the door it merged with, and any marks are left on the door once 1455 leaves.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Generally, I like it.

My only suggestion for improvement would be to have 1455 merge with the new door, instead of replace it. That way, it leaves a door behind it when it leaves the frame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I somewhat enjoy it, but the thought of a door hopping about is extremely silly. I'll think about this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept in general still refuses to interest me. It's made even worse by the fact that I still have the taste of 1022 in my mouth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And in fact, I was inspired by 1022.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoever wrote SCP-1022 should get over here and read this. This is how you do this concept well.
